items.title: Daniel Turpin named as diver killed in Albany shark attack

children.content: The family of a 35-year-old diver who died after being bitten by a shark has described him as "an adored husband, son, brother and uncle who had a lifelong love and deep respect for the ocean".

articles.title: Diver dies after being bitten by 4.5m shark off Albany

children.content: A male diver has died after an attack by a suspected 4.5-metre shark near Michaelmas Island, off WA's Albany coast, on Saturday morning.

gallery.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short brown hair and a white shirt.

items.title: Obscene acts charges prompt calls for more CCTV

children.content: A woman who reported a terrifying incident while walking at night in Derby says more CCTV cameras would increase safety and help monitor popular locations.

image.altText: The main strip of Derby, a narrow road surrounded by red dirt and bushy trees.

items.title: Mechelle Turvey among 'outstanding' Australians on King's Birthday Honours roll

children.content: Almost 950 Australians are honoured, including five stand-out recipients who are now Companions of the Order — among them, the mother of murdered Noongar-Yamatji schoolboy Cassius Turvey.

image.altText: A graphic with the images of three women and two men.

items.title: Rare bird sighting excites twitchers

children.content: Birdwatchers from across Perth and even interstate have flocked to WA's Midwest after the sighting of a black-headed gull.

gallery.altText: a silver grey gull with black head wings spread in flight over a beach
Bird with dark brown head and seagull white and grey body stands on beach
Man crawls commando style on beach with large camera and gulls

items.title: Private medical imaging provider bills Medicare for non-eligible services

children.content: A private medical imaging provider operating in a public hospital has been billing Medicare for some services that are not eligible for bulk billing, according to an internal memo obtained by the ABC.

items.title: Meet the man born, raised and married in his local town hall

children.content: Brian Baker, 95, spent the first 21 years of his life in living quarters at the York Town Hall where his father was caretaker.

image.altText: Man on mobility scooter dwarfed by large ornate stage and red velvet curtains

items.title: Could this famous architect-turned-priest be named WA's first saint?

children.content: The life of a world famous architect-turned-Catholic priest is being examined to decide if he should be named Western Australia's first saint. John Cyril Hawes constructed more than a dozen famous church buildings across regional WA.

gallery.altText: Image of John Hawes sitting in a collard coat and looking at the camera. Beside him is a small dog.
Monsignor John Hawes and a worker sit on a platform
A man and his dog in statue sit on a round fountain in front of a cathedral. Bird fly in the sky.

thumbnail.altText: Image of John Hawes sitting in a collard coat and looking at the camera. Beside him is a small dog.
Monsignor John Hawes and a worker sit on a platform
A man and his dog in statue sit on a round fountain in front of a cathedral. Bird fly in the sky.

items.title: 'We blinked': Government accused of backing down on AI regulation

children.content: The world's biggest tech titans see Australia as a promising investment opportunity for their AI ambitions, but some fear the federal government's regulatory retreat could cause more harm.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and business shirt sitting in a chair during an interview with a neutral expression on his face.

thumbnail.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and business shirt sitting in a chair during an interview with a neutral expression on his face.

items.title: Top neurologists say regulator ignored warnings as weed killer decision looms

children.content: Australia's leading movement disorder specialists say the chemical regulator has not engaged with their concerns about paraquat, despite growing evidence linking the herbicide to Parkinson's disease.

image.altText: A close-up shows a man's face next to the label on the outside of a metal chemical drum

items.title: Anthropic wants caution but the market wants more AI

children.content: Anthropic wants caution as AI intelligence surges but the stock market can't get enough.

image.altText: Anthropic logo on a smartphone screen

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: From migration to AUKUS, more politicians are speaking out

children.content: Former Coalition frontbencher Paul Scarr says inflammatory migration rhetoric is taking a toll on migrant communities. His warning comes as both major parties wrestle with populist pressures.

image.altText: Man standing in a suit in the senate

items.title: The electric classic car that now drives like a 'pocket rocket'

children.content: It may look like a small vintage car, but an EV conversion has brought it "up to speed" to its modern counterparts.

image.altText: David Dyer kneeling next to blue small classic car

items.title: Farmers question plan to build youth centre near Ord cotton properties

children.content: The decision to locate Kununurra's new youth centre on an abandoned farm 7km from town has sparked local concerns.

gallery.altText: A grey shed surrounded by long grass and trees
A bale of cotton on farmland with red hills in background
An aerial of the Ord Irrigation Scheme

items.title: History quiz: When was the first Mars Exploration Rover launch?

children.content: What make of car was stolen in the world's first official vehicle theft? And do you know where the Endeavour was repaired after hitting the Great Barrier Reef in 1770?

image.altText: A black and white image of a robot arm surveying the rocky, barren surface of Mars.

items.title: Aussie pole vaulter hands Duplantis first loss in more than 1,000 days

children.content: Kurtis Marschall pulls off a major upset, defeating world record holder Armand "Mondo" Duplantis at the Stockholm Diamond League meet.

image.altText: Kurtis Marschall celebrates winning the men's pole vault at the Stockholm Diamond League meet.

items.title: Quick hits: Monaco GP interrupted for safety as Kardashians keep up with F1

children.content: From a dodgy section of track to a mega celebrity watching her partner equal a record with the great Ayrton Senna, the 2026 Monaco Grand Prix had plenty of talking points.

image.altText: Kim Kardashian looks on in parc ferme at Monaco

items.title: Piastri promoted after penalty as teen sensation wins wild Monaco GP

children.content: Teenage sensation Kimi Antonelli was flawless to lead every lap of a drama-filled Monaco Grand Prix, claiming a fifth consecutive win to extend his lead in the Formula 1 drivers' championship. Follow live.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri at the Monaco Grand Prix 2026

items.title: With his pet chihuahua and trusty gopher, 97yo grazier won't slow down

children.content: At 97, Doug Harrison still scoots around his outback station on a gopher, doing as much work as he can. As he nears a century of living, he reflects on memorable moments over the decades, with a cuppa in hand and his trusty chihuahua by his side.

gallery.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him
an old man with black sunglasses and a red face close to the camera looking

thumbnail.altText: a man with black sunglasses holding the steering wheel of a vehicle with a white small dog sitting next to him
an old man with black sunglasses and a red face close to the camera looking

items.title: Disability community says NDIS 'fighting the wrong battles' over funding plans

children.content: The agency spent $60 million on lawyers to fight participants at a tribunal last financial year. Disability advocates say there should be greater focus on the NDIS's legal fees as cuts to the scheme are being made.

image.altText: A man and a woman both in wheelchair going down a walkway with trees

items.title: Zverev claims first major with French Open victory

children.content: In his fourth appearance in the final of a major tournament, Alexander Zverev lifts the French Open trophy following a five-set grind against Flavio Cobolli.

image.altText: Alexander Zverev of Germany kisses the Coupe des Mousquetaires trophy at the French Open.

items.title: Blues beat Dons to make it four in a row, Swans pip Saints in a thriller

children.content: Carlton continues its resurgence under interim coach Josh Fraser with a five-point victory over Essendon, after Sydney completes a thrilling victory over St Kilda.

image.altText: An AFL player in dark blue celebrates a goal, framed by players in red and black

items.title: NSW woman claims ignorance of plot to smuggle fugitive bikie out of Australia

children.content: Fatma Skaf faced the Karratha Magistrates Court today over her alleged involvement in a plot to spirit Queensland bikie Dylan Cvek out of the country on a luxury yacht.

image.altText: A person wearing police/army uniform in a boat looking over at white yacht

items.title: Biggest universal magnetic map unlocks new chapter of space research

children.content: Data gathered by scientists at the Square Kilometre Array has resulted in a new "map" detailing the magnetic web of our observable universe.

gallery.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.

thumbnail.altText: A generated image shows cloud-like plumes of vibrant red and blue of varying brightness against a black background.
